schema_notes: Per-billionaire record matching Ellison v3 reference. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification downstream.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research (Huang) + regen_v3@sha256-99da3c801e8db2fe + regen_v3@sha256-5a6a8a1a9b600621 + regen_v3@sha256-1fbba143ceeff3ce

person.wealth_source: Nvidia Corp. co-founder and CEO since 1993; ~3% ownership stake in Nvidia (world's most valuable company as of 2026). Wealth is concentrated in Nvidia stock held in his name, in a family trust, and via the Huang Foundation + GeForce Fund DAF.
person.years_as_billionaire_note: Huang crossed the $1B line publicly in ~2017 (Forbes listed him at ~$3B in 2019). Was worth only $4.7B in the 2020 Forbes list and $13.5B at start of 2023. The AI boom from 2023-2026 took him from a ~$20B peripheral figure to top-10 globally ($124B in Oct 2024, $164-178B by April 2026). Treat tenure as ~9 years for formal purposes; economically meaningful billionaire tenure is ~3 years. This materially lowers the tenure-weighted expected-giving denominator.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 profile ~$164B (rank #8); Visual Capitalist top-20 2026 ~$162.5B; techi.com real-time $178B; indmoney real-time Bloomberg ~$176B. Spread reflects daily Nvidia stock moves. Central estimate $170B.

sources.trajectory_note: Forbes 2020 list: $4.7B. 2023 start: $13.5B. May 2024: $90B. Oct 2024: $124B. 2026-04: ~$164-178B. Illustrates AI-boom surge.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Huang's wealth is more liquid than Ellison's: ~3% of Nvidia is a smaller concentration ownership stake than Ellison's 41% of Oracle, and Huang has been actively selling shares under 10b5-1 plans. But Nvidia is still his dominant asset and the stock is volatile. Apply 35% liquidity discount for 'giving capacity now'; use 70% for lifetime 'expected giving' denominator.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. Unlike Ellison, Huang's political giving is negligible and does NOT displace charitable giving in any material way.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Estimate. Inside Philanthropy Oct 2021 noted 'neither of the Huangs have been notable political donors.' Yahoo Finance Nov 2024 noted Huang 'kept his hands clean of political endorsements' in the 2024 election. FEC API direct-pull not run; publicly available OpenSecrets records show no major super-PAC activity. Floor $0; upper estimate $500K reflects occasional individual candidate contributions typical of tech CEOs.

sources.quote: Neither of the Huangs have been notable political donors, but in an interview from late 2016, Jensen Huang stated that 'on balance, I prefer a more liberal government.'

sources.note: 2024 election: Huang 'kept his hands clean of political endorsements either towards red or blue.'

foundations_active.name: The Jen-Hsun and Lori Huang Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (Jen-Hsun Huang serves as Secretary/CFO; Lori Huang serves as President; both receive zero compensation per 990-PF)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($126.3M) vs. 2024 assets ($9.21B) = 1.37% — BELOW the 5% legal minimum. Required distribution for 2025 is ~$369M (FoundationMark estimate). Foundation will need to dramatically increase giving or face 30% excise tax. FLAG: LOW_PAYOUT.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: CONFIRMED: 65% of 2024 payout ($82.5M of $126.3M) transferred to the GeForce Fund DAF at DAFgiving360/Schwab Charitable — a DAF that the Huangs themselves control. 'Vast majority' of 2023 payout also went to the same DAF per Fortune. FLAG: DAF_TRANSFERS (historically 80%+, recently dropped to 65%).

dafs_detected.name: GeForce Fund
dafs_detected.balance_note: Regulatory filings show Schwab Charitable controls ~$4B of Nvidia shares, 'at least some of which are held in GeForce' (per Business Times / Fortune). Total GeForce Fund balance is not publicly disclosed — DAFs are donor-opaque. Assume GeForce holds low-single-digit billions.
dafs_detected.classification_note: This is a DONOR-CONTROLLED DAF, not an external DAF. Dollars transferred from JHLHF to GeForce are intra-vehicle — they have NOT yet reached an operating nonprofit. Per IPS methodology, exclude from observable giving. Only count GeForce's grants OUT to operating charities (not disclosed; unknowable from public data).

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: Huang's DAF activity is substantial and publicly flagged by Inside Philanthropy / Fortune / NYT. Key issue: grants OUT of GeForce Fund to operating charities are NOT disclosed. ~$82M/year flows IN to GeForce via JHLHF; what flows OUT is unknowable.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC identified. No CZI-style structure.
detected_vehicles.for_profit_hybrid_note: Unlike Ellison's EIT, Huang has no for-profit 'hybrid' philanthropy vehicle. All documented charitable activity flows through the 501(c)(3) foundation or the DAF.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. No public reporting of offshore philanthropic structure.

trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.source_quote: SEC filings show transfers from 'the couple's trust' (e.g., 440,000 Nvidia shares, ~$60M, to JHLHF in June 2025).
trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.classification_note: Structure not disclosed. Unknown whether grantor trust, CRT, CLT, or dynasty trust. Trust appears to function as holding vehicle for Huang's personal Nvidia shares with periodic charitable transfers. NYT Dec 2024 estimates Nvidia shares transferred to the foundation + other charitable vehicles (including GeForce) total ~$2B.

red_flags.evidence: JHLHF 2024 grants_out ($126.3M) / 2024 assets ($9.21B) = 1.37%, below 5% IRS minimum. Foundation must distribute ~$369M in 2025 per FoundationMark estimate to avoid excise tax. Asset growth has outpaced grantmaking growth by ~10x since 2022.

65% of 2024 foundation payout ($82.5M of $126.3M) flowed to the Huangs' own GeForce Fund DAF at Schwab Charitable. Historically higher (Inside Philanthropy: 80%+ threshold to qualify as 'Black Box Billionaire' — Huang crossed back under in 2024 but only barely). Net 'out-the-door' giving is 35% of foundation payout.

Net worth grew from $4.7B (Forbes 2020) to $164B (Forbes 2026) — a ~35x increase in 6 years. Foundation grants_out grew from $13.8M (2020) to $126.3M (2024) — ~9x. Asset growth of the foundation itself (from $828M to $9.2B, ~11x) outstripped grantmaking pace.

red_flags_not_present: GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED — Huang has NOT signed the Giving Pledge, so there is no fulfillment gap to flag. Different from Ellison.
POLITICAL_DOMINATES_CHARITABLE — Huang has minimal political giving (~$500K lifetime upper estimate). Charitable giving dwarfs political.
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP — Both Huangs receive zero comp from foundation.

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(JHLHF 990-PF grants_out 2020-2024) = $13.8M + $31.5M + $66.4M + $59.8M + $126.3M = $297.8M. Plus pre-2020 cumulative (Stanford $30M 2008 + OHSU $5M + Oneida $2M + other ~$5M) ≈ $40M. Plus CCA 2025 $22.5M (not yet in 990-PF but press-confirmed). Total ≈ $360M. Subtract the $82.5M 2024 transfer to Huangs' own GeForce Fund DAF (not yet out-the-door), net $278M. ROUND to $338M INCLUDING DAF transfers (gross); $255M net-of-DAF-to-self. Use $338M as gross observable for comparability with Ellison's methodology (Ellison DAF transfers not documented; Huang's are explicit so we show both).
observable_giving_todo: Pull JHLHF 2025 990-PF when filed (expected late 2026) to confirm CCA $22.5M, SF Opera $5M, and compliance with IRS $369M required distribution.
Attempt to identify GeForce Fund grantees via recipient-side 990 filings (search operating charities for 'GeForce Fund' or 'Schwab Charitable Fund — Huang' as a donor).
Run SEC EDGAR Form 4 G-transactions for Huang CIK to cross-check foundation contribution totals.
Confirm Stanford $30M was paid in full (pledged 2008; building dedicated; no public default).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.35 × $170B = $59.5B; tenure factor = min(9/10, 1) = 0.9. Expected = 0.10 × $59.5B × 0.9 = $5.36B. NOTE: using formal-tenure of 9 years. Using economically-meaningful tenure of 3 years (AI-boom era): factor = 0.30; expected = $1.79B. The formal-tenure number is the right one for list-comparability; the economic-tenure number is the right one for the 'is this person actually a Scrooge' question. Show both.
rollup.expected_giving_method_note: The gap between formal and economic tenure is itself the story for Huang: he has been a billionaire on paper for ~9 years but has been in the top-10 only since ~late 2024. His giving is ramping — foundation payout nearly doubled 2023→2024 — but not fast enough relative to the asset surge.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: Biggest single hidden-upper component. The NYT Dec 2024 investigation estimated ~$2B in Nvidia shares transferred to the foundation + other charitable vehicles (including GeForce Fund DAF). GeForce Fund's grants-out to operating charities are NOT disclosed. Upper bound: if GeForce has paid out its entire balance, the $2B uplift would be legitimate. Lower bound (and likely reality): most of GeForce is still sitting in the DAF, generating tax-deferred appreciation without reaching operating charities. Cap the uplift at $2B for the upper envelope.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Huang's profile (quiet, low-profile — InfluenceWatch: 'very low profile') suggests above-cohort-median anonymous giving likelihood. Apply: 0.1% of liquid_wealth = 0.001 × $170B × 0.35 = $60M as baseline. Widen to $170M upper bound to capture possible anonymous grants from GeForce Fund not otherwise attributable.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Huang attended Oneida Baptist Institute as a child and gave $2M to the school, but no adult religious-donor pattern evidenced.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_note: Unlike Ellison, Huang has not publicly declared his trust is for charitable purposes. The trust appears to be a standard family holding trust with ad-hoc charitable transfers. Zero uncertainty envelope — we do not credit undisclosed trust assets as charitable intent.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2 under the formal-tenure denominator. Huang's ratio is 0.47 — above the 0.20 threshold, so he is NOT Tier A. But he is clearly below the 10% norm under formal tenure (6.3% gross, 4.8% net-of-DAF). Tier B is the right classification: meaningful giving ramp, structural LOW_PAYOUT + DAF_TRANSFERS red flags, substantial ~$2B in pre-positioned charitable dollars still inside donor-controlled vehicles.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Huang as a 'recently-minted mega-billionaire whose giving infrastructure has not scaled to match his wealth.' The foundation is actively ramping ($126M in 2024, required ~$369M in 2025). But structural issues remain: 65% of foundation payout goes to his own DAF, not to operating charities; GeForce Fund grantees are not disclosed; LOW_PAYOUT flag is active. Under economic-tenure (3yr) analysis he looks better (ratio 1.4x expected); under formal-tenure analysis he looks worse (0.47x expected). Both views are legitimate. Do NOT classify as 'unfulfilled pledge' — he never signed the Giving Pledge.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity. Tenure penalty is low because Huang has been a billionaire only ~9 years (economically ~3). Pledge breach is zero (not a signer). Red flag penalty reflects LOW_PAYOUT + DAF_TRANSFERS + NET_WORTH_SURGE (3 flags). Giving gap: 1 - (0.338B / 3.57B effective expected) × 0.4 × 100 = 37.5. DO NOT PUBLISH — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.

right_of_reply.planned_outreach_route: Nvidia Investor Relations + Jen-Hsun and Lori Huang Foundation (c/o Frank Rimerman & Co LLP, Palo Alto) + Huang personal office at Nvidia HQ.

VERIFY: FEC bulk pull against Huang + spouse names — confirm or refine <$500K political floor.
